title: Sinarundinaria ferax
taxonomy: Sinarundinaria ferax is a species of bamboo native to China. It belongs to the family Poaceae and is classified within the genus Sinarundinaria. Sinarundinaria ferax is further divided into several subspecies, with varying characteristics such as leaf shape and size. This bamboo species is commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions, often in well-drained areas with plenty of sunlight. In terms of its classification, Sinarundinaria ferax is a monocot, meaning it has one cotyledon during germination.
